attributes.description: Alerts when any old-generation (full) GC cycles occur (`java_lang_G1_Old_Generation_CollectionCount` increases). Full GCs stop-the-world and should be rare in a healthy JVM.
attributes.name: [Tomcat OTel] Old generation GC activity

investigation_guide.blob:
## Tomcat old generation GC activity

### What fired
`java_lang_G1_Old_Generation_CollectionCount` increased during the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Old-generation collections (mixed / full GCs under G1) pause the entire JVM. Even a single full GC can cause request-latency spikes lasting hundreds of milliseconds to seconds. Frequent full GCs indicate heap pressure that young-gen collection cannot resolve.

### Triage
1. JVM Memory & GC dashboard: look at collection count and collection time trend.
2. Inspect `java_lang_G1_Old_Gen_Usage_used` post-GC baseline — rising means memory leak.
3. Correlate with heap utilization and request latency spikes.
4. If redeploys coincide, suspect classloader leaks.

### Remediation
- Fix memory leaks revealed by heap dumps.
- Size heap correctly — too small drives frequent full GCs, too large extends pause duration.
- Tune G1 region size / IHOP if old-gen pressure is structural.

### Tuning
- Fires on any full GC. For heavy batch workloads where occasional old-gen GCs are acceptable, set a rate threshold.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-tomcat.otel-*
| WHERE java_lang_G1_Old_Generation_CollectionCount IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    max_count = MAX(java_lang_G1_Old_Generation_CollectionCount),
    min_count = MIN(java_lang_G1_Old_Generation_CollectionCount)
  BY host.name
| EVAL full_gc_count = max_count - min_count
// Any old generation (full) GC pauses the entire JVM and directly impacts latency
// Frequent full GC (> 1/min) is critical — adjust threshold to match your GC tuning
| WHERE full_gc_count > 0
| SORT full_gc_count DESC
